notes: Server 3.39 retunes the Compatibility scan to flag only files that genuinely won't play on most devices. Dropped the '10-bit HEVC SDR' rule, which was ~78% of all flags — 10-bit HEVC (Main10) plays on essentially every modern client, and re-encoding it to 8-bit H.264 is lossy and pointless. Compat now flags only Hi10P (10-bit H.264), legacy codecs (VC-1/MPEG-2/MPEG-4-ASP/WMV/etc.), non-MKV/MP4 containers (e.g. AVI), interlaced, and subtitle overload (>12 tracks — the likely cause of the Euphoria stalls, fixed by a lossless sub-trim). Existing over-flagged entries were re-evaluated and cleared. Node 2.33 (mkvmerge exit-1 fix + analyze timeout) and server 3.38 (auto-requeue transient errors) still apply — update both nodes to 2.33.
